claims.claim: The highest flame propagation velocity of LIB vent gases in a pipeline was about 20.4 m/s, occurring at approximately 12.5 ms during the spherical flame stage.
claims.quote: the flame velocity increased sharply to about 20.4 m/s in 12.5 ms. This corresponds to the spherical flame stage and the flame could propagate freely.

claims.claim: The highest overpressure of LIB vent gases flame propagation in a pipeline was about 0.595 MPa, which was lower than the overpressure of pure H2 or pure CH4 in air (about 0.7 MPa) due to the presence of inert gas in LIB vent gases.
claims.quote: The highest overpressure was about 0.595 MPa. This value was lower than the overpressure of pure H2 or pure CH4 in air (about 0.7 MPa), it was mainly because of the LIB vent gases including inert gas.

claims.claim: In the energy storage container explosion simulation, the maximum explosion overpressure inside the container was about 23 kPa, because the venting of pressure relief panels and escape door greatly decreased the overpressure.
claims.quote: the maximum explosion overpressure reached 23.19 kPa inside the container... The venting of pressure relief panels and door could greatly decrease the overpressure.

claims.claim: In the energy storage container explosion, the pressure relief panels opened when internal pressure reached 3 kPa, and the escape door opened when internal pressure reached 20 kPa.
claims.quote: At t = 0.0695 s, the pressure inside the box reached to 3 kPa, so the pressure relief panels opened... At t = 0.1417 s, the pressure inside the box reached 20 kPa, causing the door to open.

claims.claim: The maximum damage distance caused by explosion pressure of LIB vent gases in an energy storage container was about 10 m, based on a minimum damage threshold of 4.8 kPa overpressure.
claims.quote: Based on previous studies [46–48], 4.8 kPa was selected as the threshold for the minimum damage. The result showed that the overpressure of P8, P9 and P10 was close to 4.8 kPa, while the overpressure of P3, P4 and P5 was smaller than the threshold. It meant that the maximum distance of damage was about 10 m.

claims.claim: The thickness of the distribution of free radicals decreased with the flame propagation process in the pipeline, and lower free radical concentration corresponded to lower flame propagation velocity.
claims.quote: the thickness of the distribution of free radicals was decreasing with the flame propagation process. This corresponded to the variety of flame velocity, the lower the concentration of free radicals, the lower the flame propagation velocity.

claims.claim: At a LIB vent gas concentration of about 20%, the explosion pressure was maximized and the rate of pressure rise was also at a high level.
claims.quote: The results show that at a concentration of about 20 %, not only is the explosion pressure of the LIB vent gases maximized, but the rate of pressure rise is also at a high level.

claims.claim: The high temperature zone of LIB vent gases jet flame resulted from pyrolysis of CH3 radicals and reaction of OH radicals, with the top of the high temperature zone coinciding with OH distribution and the bottom coinciding with CH3 distribution.
claims.quote: The result indicated that the top of high temperature zone coincided with the location of OH distribution, while the bottom of high temperature zone coincided with the location of CH3 distribution. This demonstrated that the high temperature zone resulted from pyrolysis of CH3 radicals and reaction of OH radicals.

claims.claim: The explosion risk of the door side of the energy storage container was higher than that of the pressure relief panels side, because the larger area of the door allowed more gases to be vented through it.
claims.quote: the risk of door side was higher than that of the relief panels side. This is because the larger area of door, so the more gases could be vented through the door.

claims.claim: The jet flame height of LIB vent gases increased with the increase of state of charge (SOC).
claims.quote: Mao [20] also analyzed the jet flame of LIB vent gases with different state of charge (SOC) in open space and found that the height of the jet flame increased with the increase of SOC.

claims.claim: No thermal runaway propagation occurred in NCM622 battery modules when the SOC was below 25% in closed space.
claims.quote: no thermal runaway occurred in the battery module when the state of charge was below 25% in closed space.

claims.claim: When SOC decreased from 100% to 50%, the total gas production volume of NCM622 battery modules decreased from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol.
claims.quote: The total gas production volume from 17.48 mol to 6.17 mol when the state of charge was reduced from 100% to 50%.

claims.claim: With decreasing SOC, CO2 content in vent gas increased from 38.91% to 70.45%, while CO content decreased from 23.91% to 0.06% and C2H4 content decreased from 8.92% to 1.15%.
claims.quote: as the SOC decreased, the content of CO2 increases from 38.91% to 70.45%. The content of CO and C2H4 decreased with the decrease of SOC, from 23.91% and 8.92–0.06% and 1.15% respectively.

claims.claim: When SOC decreased from 100% to 50%, the average thermal runaway propagation time between batteries in the module increased from 96.93 s to 221.88 s.
claims.quote: The average propagation times between modules at 100–50% SOC were 96.93 s, 162.85 s, and 221.88 s, respectively.

claims.claim: When SOC decreased from 100% to 25%, the internal thermal runaway propagation speed of battery #1 decreased from 4.06 mm/s to 1.15 mm/s, the maximum surface temperature decreased from 927.2°C to 486.6°C, and the mass loss rate decreased from 24.06 g/s to 8.7 g/s.
claims.quote: When the SOC decreased from 100% to 25%, the highest temperature on the battery surface decreased from 927.2 ℃ to 486.6 ℃; the speed of TRP inside the battery cells decreased from 4.06 mm⋅s−1 to 1.15 mm⋅s−1; the rate of mass loss decreased from 24.06 g/s to 8.7 g/s.

claims.claim: When SOC was higher than 50%, the explosive limit range of combustible gas generated by the NCM622 battery module was between 7.64% and 56.19%, approximately 5 times the explosive limit range of CH4.
claims.quote: when the SOC was higher than 50%, the explosive limit range of combustible gas generated by the battery module was between 7.64% and 56.19%. Comparing it with the explosive limit of flammable gas CH4, it can be found that the explosive limit range of the mixed gas generated by the module was about 5 times that of CH4

claims.claim: For a 280Ah LFP prismatic cell, TR severity intensifies above 50% SOC; during 25%, 50%, and 75% SOC tests, maximum cell temperature increased sequentially by 35% and 258.3%, while gas emissions rose by 79.3% and 644.7%, respectively.
claims.quote: Results demonstrate that TR severity intensifies above 50 % SOC. During the 25 %, 50 %, and 75 % SOC tests, the maximum cell temperature increased sequentially by 35 % and 258.3 %, while gas emissions rose by 79.3 % and 644.7 %, respectively.

claims.claim: The number of gaseous components detected during thermal runaway increases with SOC: 15 components at 25% SOC, 19 at 50% SOC, and 36 at 75% SOC.
claims.quote: Furthermore, 15, 19, and 36 gaseous components are detected under the three conditions.

claims.claim: At higher SOC, vent gas composition shifts toward reduced CO2 fraction and elevated H2 fraction.
claims.quote: At higher SOCs, a notable characteristic of the gas composition is the reduction in CO2 and the elevation in H2.

claims.claim: The lower flammability limit (LFL) for vent gases at 25% and 50% SOC (10%–15%) is significantly higher than at 75% SOC (5%), indicating greater ignition susceptibility at 75% SOC.
claims.quote: The lower flammability limit for 25 % and 50 % SOC (10 %–15 %) is slightly higher than that for 75 % SOC (5 %).

claims.claim: The Flammability Hazard Index (FHI) of vent gas mixtures follows the order 50% SOC > 75% SOC > 25% SOC, indicating that flammability risk does not increase monotonically with SOC.
claims.quote: The FHI followed the order: 50 % SOC > 75 % SOC > 25 % SOC, indicating that flammability risk does not always increase monotonically with SOC.

claims.claim: At 50% SOC, 50% SOC appears to be the critical threshold for triggering thermal runaway in the tested 280Ah LFP cells; cells at 0%, 25%, and 50% SOC did not exhibit significant TR behavior after prolonged heating, reaching Tmax of 77°C, 92.3°C, and 251.1°C respectively.
claims.quote: For all tested cells, 50 % SOC appears to be the critical threshold for triggering TR. However, this issue warrants further investigation for cells with different material systems or energy densities... the cells at 0 %, 25 %, and 50 % SOC exhibited a slight temperature increase followed by a subsequent decrease, with Tmax reaching 77 ℃, 92.3 ℃, and 251.1 ℃ at approximately 5900 s, 5122 s, and 3560 s, respectively.

claims.claim: Gas production at 120%, 100%, 75%, and 50% SOC was 16.5, 13.9, 7.4, and 2.9 times that at 25% SOC, respectively, demonstrating a strong positive relationship between SOC and total gas emission.
claims.quote: gas emissions increase with SOC, with gas production at 120 %, 100 %, 75 %, and 50 % SOC being 16.5, 13.9, 7.4, and 2.9 times that at 25 % SOC, respectively.

claims.claim: The upper flammability limit (UFL) for vent gases at 25% and 50% SOC (55%–65%) is significantly higher than at 75% SOC (4.7%–6.6%), and the flammable range (RF) at 25%, 50%, and 75% SOC are 43.7%, 48.5%, and 17.6%, respectively.
claims.quote: The upper flammability limit for 25 % and 50 % SOC (55 %–65 %) is significantly higher than that for 75 % SOC (4.7 %–6.6 %)... The RF corresponding to 25 %, 50 %, and 75 % SOC are approximately 43.7 %, 48.5 %, and 17.6 %, respectively.

claims.claim: As SOC increases from 0% to 120%, the maximum vent gas temperature increases from 100.7°C to 418.8°C, following a linear relationship described by Maxgas_temp = 2.9 × SOC + 80.75 (R² = 0.97827).
claims.quote: During tests at 0–120 % SOC, the maximum gas temperatures reached 100.7 ℃, 120 ℃, 235.5 ℃, 315.9 ℃, 379.9 ℃, and 418.8 ℃, respectively... Maxgas_temp = 2.9 × SOC +80.75 (R2 = 0.97827)

claims.claim: The proportion of CO2 in vent gas decreases with increasing SOC, from approximately 61% at 25% SOC to 40% at 50% SOC and 12% at 75% SOC, attributed to altered electrolyte decomposition pathways at higher lithium concentrations.
claims.quote: The proportion of CO2 decreases with increasing SOC (from 61 % to 40–12 %), a trend attributed to the alterations in the decomposition pathways of the electrolyte. At higher SOCs, the increased lithium concentration within the cell promotes reactions between the active materials and the electrolyte, resulting in a preference for the production of H2 or hydrocarbons.
